proof_sketch: The mutation modifies `tid < stride` to `tid < stride - 1` in the reduction loop of the non-affine kernel (`instance_norm_forward_kernel`). The host code calls this kernel only when `weight.defined() && bias.defined()` is false. In the test contract, the model uses `affine=True` by default, so both weight and bias are defined (Parameters), causing the affine kernel (`instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine`) to be invoked instead. The non-affine kernel is never executed. Therefore the mutated code is dead code under the fixed configuration.

In the test contract, the model uses `affine=True` by default, so both weight and bias are defined (Parameters), causing the affine kernel (`instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine`) to be invoked instead. The non-affine kernel is never executed. Therefore the mutated code is dead code under the fixed configuration.",

Therefore the mutant is unkillable under the given fixed-shape contract.",
